design_philosophy.core_loop: Observe opponent attack patterns → counter-punch at precise moments → build stars for powerful uppercut → repeat until knockout. Each opponent has a fixed, learnable pattern.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Pattern recognition vs. execution timing. Player must identify visual tells in opponent behavior, predict attacks, and respond with frame-perfect inputs while managing stamina (heart counter).
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Underdog boxing ascension: playing as Little Mac (107 lbs, 17 years old) climbing from Minor Circuit through World Circuit to face legendary heavyweight Mike Tyson.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately restrictive moveset (only jabs, body blows, uppercut) forces player mastery through limitation rather than options. Victory requires memorization and pattern reading, not reflexes alone. The game rejects button-mashing; it punishes it. Each opponent recycles sprite data with new attack patterns, emphasizing that the challenge is intellectual pattern-solving, not visual variety.

difficulty.lives_system: Mac can be knocked down up to 3 times per bout before losing by KO. Fourth knockdown ends bout in loss.
difficulty.continues_system: Loss to ranked opponent allows one rematch. Loss in Title Bout causes ranking drop (1-2 places depending on circuit). Third loss or any loss in Dream Fight ends the game.
difficulty.checkpointing: Progression is circuit-based (Minor → Major → World → Dream Fight). Winning circuit championship advances to next circuit. Ranking system creates soft checkpoints; losing demotes player within circuit.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No difficulty settings documented. Player can grind rematches to learn patterns before advancing. Bout outcomes (KO/TKO/Decision) vary; some opponents cannot be won by decision, forcing knockouts. Heart counter mechanic creates resource tension: attacking costs hearts, blocking costs hearts, getting hit costs hearts.

progression.upgrade_system: Star system: counter-punching opponents at correct moments earns up to 3 stars. Stars enable powerful uppercut attack. Stars reset on knockdown or successful opponent hit. Hearts (stamina) regenerate passively by dodging/avoiding attacks.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Circuit advancement: 3 Minor Circuit opponents → win Minor Championship → 4 Major Circuit opponents → win Major Championship → 6 World Circuit opponents (including 3 repeated from earlier circuits with new patterns) → win World Championship → Dream Fight vs. Mike Tyson/Mr. Dream. Linear gating; no branching.
replayability_hooks: Opponent rematch system: failing ranked match forces immediate replay to retry pattern learning
Three-loss rule creates urgency and repeat playthroughs from earlier points
World Circuit recycled opponents with new attack patterns incentivizes relearning strategy
Mike Tyson/Mr. Dream as final challenge: notorious difficulty spike encourages mastery runs

world_building_techniques: Opponent roster diversity (Glass Joe from France, Piston Honda from Japan, Von Kaiser from Germany): implied global boxing circuit context
Ring entrance music from fighters' countries of origin: reinforces cultural/national boxing traditions
Little Mac character framing: 17-year-old 107 lb underdog vs. heavyweight opponents establishes power fantasy asymmetry
Ranking system with circuit names (Minor/Major/World): hierarchical progression suggests legitimate boxing organization
Mike Tyson inclusion (1987 version): contemporaneous reference to real-world heavyweight champion adds cultural resonance

boss_design.philosophy: Each opponent is a pattern puzzle to solve. Visual tells (head movements, stance changes, animations) telegraph attacks. Victory requires identifying the tell → predicting the punch → executing the counter-punch at the frame-perfect moment. Difficulty escalates: early opponents have obvious patterns; later opponents (especially World Circuit repeats) have faster/more complex variations.
notable_bosses: Glass Joe (Minor Circuit): intentionally simplistic opponent; teaches pattern recognition to player
Bald Bull (Major and World Circuits): iconic 'Bull Charge' attack; World Circuit version adds complexity
King Hippo (World Circuit): unique sprite design (not reused); only opponent where pattern vulnerabilities are visual rather than attack-timing based
Mike Tyson / Mr. Dream (Dream Fight): final boss; reputation for extreme difficulty due to pattern complexity and compressed animation timing

faction_indicators: National/cultural identity through opponent roster (French, German, Japanese, British, Italian, Spanish, Native American, Chinese, American fighters)
danger_signals: Ring entrance music cueing: cultural stereotype associations (Wagner for German opponents, folk music for non-Western fighters)
environmental_storytelling: Opponent introductions with entrance music establish boxing arena as cosmopolitan international venue
Little Mac's size disadvantage (107 lbs vs. heavyweight opponents) creates implicit narrative of underprivileged challenger
Ranking system with circuit levels suggests hierarchical, merit-based boxing organization where advancement is earned through victory
